omia 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Analyses
    • Cat BA Week 1 Analysis

Cat BA Week 1 Analysis

cat-ba-week1-analysis.Rmd
library(omia)
library(ggplot2)

Data 

data(cat_ba_week1)
cat_ba_week1
#> # A tibble: 23 × 3
#>      day date       incidence
#>    <int> <date>         <dbl>
#>  1     1 2024-01-01         1
#>  2     2 2024-01-02         0
#>  3     3 2024-01-03         1
#>  4     4 2024-01-04         4
#>  5     5 2024-01-05         1
#>  6     6 2024-01-06         2
#>  7     7 2024-01-07         0
#>  8     8 2024-01-08         0
#>  9     9 2024-01-09         3
#> 10    10 2024-01-10         2
#> # ℹ 13 more rows

Prepare incidence data 

inc <- prep_incidence_data(cat_ba_week1)
inc
#> # A tibble: 23 × 2
#>    date       incidence
#>    <date>         <dbl>
#>  1 2024-01-01         1
#>  2 2024-01-02         0
#>  3 2024-01-03         1
#>  4 2024-01-04         4
#>  5 2024-01-05         1
#>  6 2024-01-06         2
#>  7 2024-01-07         0
#>  8 2024-01-08         0
#>  9 2024-01-09         3
#> 10 2024-01-10         2
#> # ℹ 13 more rows

Plot epidemic curve 

plot_epicurve(inc)

Fit growth model 

growth <- fit_poisson_growth(inc)
growth$estimates
#> # A tibble: 1 × 6
#>   term  growth_rate std_error conf_low conf_high doubling_time
#>   <chr>       <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>         <dbl>
#> 1 day         0.109    0.0196   0.0709     0.148          6.34

Renewal expected incidence 

si <- c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.25, 0.15)
renewal_tbl <- renewal_expected_incidence(
  incidence = inc$incidence,
  rt = 1.2,
  serial_interval = si
)
head(renewal_tbl)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 4
#>     day infectiousness    rt expected_incidence
#>   <int>          <dbl> <dbl>              <dbl>
#> 1     1           0      1.2               0   
#> 2     2           0.1    1.2               0.12
#> 3     3           0.2    1.2               0.24
#> 4     4           0.4    1.2               0.48
#> 5     5           0.85   1.2               1.02
#> 6     6           1.35   1.2               1.62

Simulate Poisson process 

sims <- simulate_poisson_process(renewal_tbl$expected_incidence, n_sims = 3, seed = 42)
head(sims)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 4
#>     sim   day expected_incidence incidence
#>   <int> <int>              <dbl>     <int>
#> 1     1     1               0            0
#> 2     1     2               0.12         1
#> 3     1     3               0.24         1
#> 4     1     4               0.48         0
#> 5     1     5               1.02         2
#> 6     1     6               1.62         2

Consensus summary 

outbreak_consensus_summary(
  incidence_data = inc,
  rt_estimates = tibble::tibble(),
  growth_fit = growth
)
#> # A tibble: 1 × 11
#>   start_date end_date   days_observed total_incidence peak_date  peak_incidence
#>   <date>     <date>             <int>           <dbl> <date>              <dbl>
#> 1 2024-01-01 2024-01-23            23              79 2024-01-23             12
#> # ℹ 5 more variables: latest_rt <dbl>, growth_rate <dbl>, doubling_time <dbl>,
#> #   trend <chr>, best_model <chr>

Optional Rt estimation with EpiEstim

If EpiEstim is installed, use:

rt <- estimate_rt_epiestim(inc)
plot_rt_estimates(rt)

On this page
